title: When Things Break 💥
description: Understand what an Exception is and why apps magically crash.

slides.title: The Unpredictable World 🌪️
slides.content:
# Code is Fragile

You write perfect code. But the real world is chaotic.

What happens when your app tries to download a picture, but the user's phone suddenly loses Wi-Fi?
What happens when your code tries to divide a number by zero?

Dart panics. It throws an **Exception**, and if you aren't prepared for it... your entire app instantly crashes.

slides.title: A Deadly Mistake 💀
slides.content: Look at this seemingly innocent code below. Can you spot the danger?

slides.title: The Red Screen of Death 🧨
slides.content:
## What is an Exception?

An Exception is Dart's way of screaming: "I don't know what to do!"

When `int.parse('hello')` fails, Dart creates a special `FormatException` object and throws it into the air.
If your code doesn't "catch" that thrown object, it hits the core engine, immediately terminating the app.

quizData.question: What is an Exception in Dart?

options.text: A special variable used for loops.
An error object thrown by the system when something unexpected and fatal happens.
A technique to speed up the app.

quizData.explanation: Exceptions represent catastrophic failures in logic or environment (like losing internet or parsing bad data).
